description: Miscellaneous: NOP, SYNC, CWAI, SWI, ABX

instructions.notes: Does nothing for 2 cycles. Used for timing loops, code patching placeholders, and alignment.
B is treated as unsigned (0-255). No flags affected. Equivalent to LEAX B,X but faster (3 vs 4+ cycles) and does not affect Z. Useful for indexing into a table using B as an unsigned offset.

instructions.operation: A ← sign extend of B  (A ← $FF if B7=1, else A ← $00)
instructions.notes: Sign extends the 8-bit value in B into the 16-bit D register. If B is negative (bit 7 set), A is set to $FF; otherwise A is cleared. Allows treating B as a signed 16-bit value in D.

instructions.full_name: AND Condition Codes and Wait for Interrupt
instructions.operation: CC ← CC AND M; push all registers; wait for interrupt
instructions.notes: Clears selected CC bits via AND mask, then pushes all registers (sets E flag in CC), then halts execution until an unmasked interrupt arrives. Faster interrupt response than SYNC because all registers are pre-saved. Minimum 20 cycles before interrupt vector is fetched.

Halts execution and waits for any interrupt. If the interrupt is masked, execution resumes at the next instruction after 2 cycles (without servicing the interrupt). If unmasked, the interrupt is serviced normally. Used for precise timing synchronization.
Pushes all registers with E set, then vectors through $FFFA/$FFFB.
Page 2 instruction. Vectors through $FFF4/$FFF5. Under OS-9 Level II this is the user-mode syscall.
